What can I redeem Avion points on? Redeem on travel, such as: Flights Hotel stays Car rentals Cruises Vacation packages Tours Converting points to other loyalty and frequent flyer programs such as WestJet Rewards, British Airways Executive Club and American Airlines AAdvantage You can also redeem points for: Gift cards Brand-name merchandise from top brands like Apple and Best Buy RBC Financial Rewards ® Charitable donations Points conversion to other loyalty programs Pay with points For more about Avion Rewards™, including complete terms and conditions, visit avionrewards.com . How do I redeem Avion points? To redeem your points, sign into Avion Rewards online or through the app, then select either “Travel “or “Redeem” in the nav bar or menu. For more information and to redeem for flights, visit avionrewards.com/travel . How do I convert Avion Rewards into other loyalty program's points, miles or partner rewards? Avion points can be converted into American Airlines AAdvantage miles, British Airways Avios and WestJet points. To find out more, sign in to Avion Rewards and visit the Convert Points section . Does the RBC Avion Visa Infinite card include travel insurance? Your RBC Avion Visa Infinite card comes with a suite of extensive travel insurance 8 , including: Out of Province/Country Emergency Medical Insurance Travel Accident Insurance Auto Rental Collision/Loss Damage Waiver Insurance Trip Cancellation & Trip Interruption Insurance Delayed Baggage and Flight Delay Insurance Hotel/Motel Burglary Insurance Purchase Security & Extended Warranty Insurance Mobile Device Insurance For more details regarding all available insurances on this card, click here . What is the Air Travel Redemption schedule? Redeem for flights using a fixed amount of Avion points based on the destination through the Air Travel Redemption Grid. Destinations have a maximum ticket price. If a ticket costs more than the allowed amount, you can charge the difference to your RBC Avion Visa Infinite card, or you can pay with points. All applicable taxes, service fees and surcharges are the responsibility of the traveller. Additional cardholders(s) (co-applicant and authorized user(s)) on the Eligible Credit Card account, as well as existing cardholders on any RBC Royal Bank personal credit card account transferring to an Eligible Credit Card account during the Offer Period, are not eligible for the offer. 35,000 welcome Avion points (“ Welcome Points ”) will appear on your monthly credit card statement within sixty (60) days after your application has been approved, provided that your Eligible Credit Card account remains open and in good standing at the time the Welcome points are credited to your account, “good standing” meaning that your Eligible Credit Card account must not be past due for more than two (2) consecutive credit card account statement periods, closed, charged off or in credit revoked status. You will receive 20,000 bonus Avion points (“ Bonus Points ”) if a total of $5,000 or more (“ Qualifying Transaction Amount ”) in qualifying net purchases and/or pre-authorized bill payment transactions (“ Qualifying Transactions ”) are posted to your Eligible Credit Card account within the first six (6) months from your Eligible Credit Card account opening date (“ Period ”) | earn 1.25 Avion points for every dollar spent when you use your RBC Avion Visa Infinite card to pay for travel-related purchases made at merchants classified by Visa's “Merchant Category Code” (“MCC”) as “travel”, which may include: airlines, accommodations, tour operators, cruise lines, travel agencies and car rental companies. What do I have to do to get the new Mobile Device insurance benefit? Nothing. The new Mobile Device insurance will automatically be applied to your WestJet RBC Mastercard as of November 5, 2025. The coverage will apply to claims incurred on or after November 5, 2025. All claims are subject to the terms, conditions, limitations and exclusions set out in the Certificates of Insurance. For there to be coverage, the mobile device must have been purchased with your WestJet RBC Mastercard card and/or WestJet points. If only a partial payment is made using WestJet points, the entire balance must be paid using your WestJet RBC Mastercard card for there to be coverage. Where can I find out more about the changes to WestJet Rewards?
